Jason Marquis

Position: Pitcher

Bats: Left  •  Throws: Right

6-1, 220lb (185cm, 99kg)

Born: August 21, 1978 in Manhasset, NY us

Draft: Drafted by the Atlanta Braves in the 1st round (35th) of the 1996 MLB June Amateur Draft from Tottenville HS (Staten Island, NY).

High School: Tottenville HS (Staten Island, NY)

Debut: June 6, 2000 (Age 21-290d, 17,679th in major league history)
   vs. TOR 1.0 IP, 0 H, 1 SO, 1 BB, 0 ER

Last Game: May 25, 2015 (Age 36-277d)
   vs. COL 5.2 IP, 10 H, 4 SO, 2 BB, 2 ER

Rookie Status: Exceeded rookie limits during 2001 season

Agents: ACES

Full Name: Jason Scott Marquis

Pronunciation: \mar-KEE\

View Player Info from the B-R Bullpen

  • 1x All-Star
  • Silver Slugger

Frequently Asked Questions

How old is Jason Marquis?

Jason Marquis is 47 years old.

When was Jason Marquis born?

Jason Marquis was born on August 21, 1978.

Where was Jason Marquis born?

Jason Marquis was born in Manhasset, NY.

How tall is Jason Marquis?

Jason Marquis is 6-1 (185 cm) tall.

How much did Jason Marquis weigh when playing?

Jason Marquis weighed 220 lbs (99 kg) when playing.

How many seasons did Jason Marquis play?

Jason Marquis played 15 seasons.

Is Jason Marquis in the Hall of Fame?

Jason Marquis has not been elected into the Hall of Fame.

When was Jason Marquis drafted?

Jason Marquis was drafted by the Atlanta Braves in the 1st round (35th) of the 1996 MLB June Amateur Draft from Tottenville HS (Staten Island, NY).

What position did Jason Marquis play?

Jason Marquis was a Pitcher.

How many strikeouts did Jason Marquis have?

Jason Marquis had 1,174 strikeouts over his career.

How many teams has Jason Marquis played for?

Jason Marquis played for 9 teams; the Arizona Diamondbacks, Atlanta Braves, Chicago Cubs, Cincinnati Reds, Colorado Rockies, Minnesota Twins, San Diego Padres, St. Louis Cardinals and Washington Nationals.

How many World Series has Jason Marquis won?

Jason Marquis has never won a World Series.

When did Jason Marquis retire?

Jason Marquis last played in 2015.
